materialChanges: Expanded the Tide-1 organismal evidence into line-specific edited-plant counts while retaining the pooled comparison and adding the missing batch-structure boundary.

Added randomized-arm denominators, observed-outcome denominators, missing-outcome reasons and handling, arm-specific missingness rates, and an extreme-case sensitivity bound for the inhibitor comparison.
Replaced the categorical confidence-interval statement with a recomputed assumption-dependent pooled interval and an explicit explanation of why it is not a design-based estimate.
Replaced learner-visible assessment JSON identifiers with descriptive Form A and Form B task labels.

reviewDispositions.rationale: The intervention row now states 100 randomized seedlings per arm, 50/96 versus 72/97 observed survivals, 4% versus 3% missingness, the operational reason for missing outcomes, and complete-case handling. The worked audit assigns all missing outcomes in both extreme directions; the inhibitor arm remains 18 to 25 percentage points lower, so it remains important but its exact effect size is explicitly uncertain.

The givens now report E1 as 24/50 and E2 as 25/50, with pooled edited survival 49/100 and control survival 82/100. The reasoning can therefore verify that both edited lines are lower than controls, while the text preserves the limitation that batch-level allocation and dependence are unavailable.

The data check now gives the assumption-dependent independent-binomial 95% interval of approximately -45 to -21 percentage points for the pooled -33-point edit-control difference, and distinguishes that sensitivity calculation from a defensible design-based interval requiring allocation, batch, and dependence information.

This note records that the source-bounded central model and all eight assessment answers were academically sound outside the identified worked-example defects. The finalizer rechecked those mappings, answer keys, scopes, and fictional/non-clinical boundaries and found no additional content change necessary.

The inhibitor example now provides totals per randomized arm, observed denominators, missingness proportions, the reason and handling for missing outcomes, and a learner-visible sensitivity analysis. Learners can evaluate rates and plausible missing-outcome bias rather than accept an unsupported claim that absolute counts are small.

Separate survival results for edited lines E1 and E2 now make the same-direction statement inspectable, and the reliability table no longer asks the learner to infer line-level consistency from a pooled total alone.
The synthesis scene now names the four tasks in ordinary learner-facing language for each form and exposes no raw assessment item identifiers.
This positive first-read finding requested no revision. The concrete Tide-1 opening, causal question, ordinary-language central move, scope, prerequisite check, and recovery route remain intact.

This positive misconception-repair finding requested no revision. Both misconception blocks still elicit a prediction, explain why the incorrect model is tempting and insufficient, replace it with a better model, compare the models, and recheck in a changed case.

evidenceChecked: All seven scenes retain one level-one heading, logical semantic source order, ordinary headings, text-first prompts and feedback, and no learner-visible project identifiers after revision.

The declarative diagram has concise alt text and an outcome-equivalent long description; the concept scene also supplies the same sequence in prose and an evidence-line table, so no essential relationship depends on vision, colour, position, motion, or hover.

Assessment and practice are untimed and text-operable, require no drag, pointer, audio, image upload, or physical procedure, allow prose, headings, bullets, tables, typing, or dictation, and provide a nonvisual dependence-map alternative.

The explicit accessibility section covers keyboard order, screen readers, narrow-screen and 200% reflow, print, reduced motion, and equivalent supplied-data routes; the safety boundary requires no personal, patient, or learner-identifying data.

Every mapped material claim resolves to a same-scene learner-visible source note and to one of four complete reference records; all source uses are factual-reference with facts-only original expression, dated rights evidence, and checked-no-agent-restriction-found access status.

The rights and attribution record declares the CC BY 4.0 contributor grant, all material authoring-agent runs, instruction digests, source-access routes, original fictional cases and data, and the absence of copied source expression or third-party assets.

The only diagram is declared original and inert, lesson.json lists no third-party assets, and the pack contains no raw HTML, JavaScript, remote embed, unsafe URL scheme, executable content, patient media, secrets, or personal health information.
The adjudicator provenance uses the supplied fresh run ID and instruction digest; that run ID appears nowhere in the authoring or review provenance.
limitations: Accessibility was audited from semantic source and structured alternatives; no live browser, screen reader, 200% zoom, narrow-device, print, or unavailable non-web export adapter was exercised in this run.

The finalizer inspected the dated source-use, licence, rights-evidence, and agent-access records but did not independently revisit external source or terms URLs; the academic review records direct source verification and the pack records checks dated 2026-07-21.

The lesson uses fictional data, so internal calculations and inference boundaries can be audited but empirical reproducibility, assay performance, target specificity, and external validity cannot be established.

rationale: The exact original candidate commit exists and precedes this run; its lesson content was unchanged before finalization apart from the later addition of the two review artifacts. Both proposed reviews match PREM-SCI-011 version 0.1.0 and candidate 90d764925745ea1a3b5bea3ac6e58666653a27f6, disclose the shared-operator conflict, use distinct run IDs and declared OpenAI and Google model families, cover the required academic and learning-design roles, and carry eligible request-changes verdicts. The operator reports equivalent non-dismissed COMMENTED GitHub transports; this run did not independently query GitHub. No review was excluded. This is disclosed and attested provenance, not proof of independent verification. The final revision resolves the shared blocking missing-data defect, the unsupported edit-line replication statement, the over-categorical interval statement, and the learner-visible internal identifiers. All nine findings are indexed above, including the three positive notes that required no change. The finalizer recomputed the percentages, extreme missing-outcome bounds, and pooled assumption-dependent interval; rechecked all assessment keys and rubrics; and completed the static accessibility, rights, provenance, privacy, and security audit. Pack-local JSON parsing and identity, mapping, evidence-note, assessment, glossary, and diagram cross-checks passed, and the review files remain unchanged. Repository npm validation was not run by explicit operator instruction, and the required temporary finalCommit literal is intentionally not schema-final until the operator substitutes the actual content commit. With those procedural conditions completed, no unresolved content, academic, learning-design, accessibility, rights, privacy, security, or provenance blocker remains, so the evidence supports merge.
dissent: Both eligible reviewers recorded request-changes rather than approval. Their overlapping major objection and all minor concerns are preserved and resolved through the indexed dispositions; they are not represented as reviewers approving the post-review revision.
conditions: Before merge, the operator must commit the final lesson content and both review artifacts, replace PENDING-FINAL-COMMIT with that exact 40-character commit SHA, and make no further teaching-content change after that commit.

After stamping the final commit, the operator must run the repository's strict source preflight and sequential validation and resolve any failure without misrepresenting this adjudication as schema-final beforehand.

The maintainer must confirm the two GitHub review transports remain equivalent and non-dismissed, then reserve any post-finalization edit to the permitted publication status, source-confidence, and retained claim review-status transition.
